questions: Modern circus ki traditional theke different?
answers: A circus is a traveling entertainment show featuring acrobats, clowns, jugglers, aerialists, and sometimes trained animals. Modern circuses like Cirque du Soleil focus on human performance artistry without animal acts.

Circus acts include trapeze, tightrope walking, contortion, aerial silks, fire breathing, juggling, clowning, and acrobatics. Performers train for years to master these dangerous and impressive skills.

Cirque du Soleil revolutionized the circus by combining acrobatics, theater, and music into spectacular artistic shows. Founded in 1984 in Canada, it has become the world's largest theatrical producer.

Modern circuses have evolved beyond the traditional big tent. They're more artistic, theatrical, and humane — most no longer use animal acts. Circus arts schools teach skills like aerial arts and acrobatics worldwide.
